Electrification of Pattukottai-Karaikudi broad gauge section nearing completion
items.summary: With the completiton of the work on the 70-km stretch, all lines  in Tiruchi Railway Division will be open to running electric locomotives; notification issued asking public in the area to be cautious

items.title: I am neither running away nor hiding: DMK leader E.V. Velu
items.summary: Displaying his medical records, Mr. Velu said he had been receiving treatment for heart ailments at Mount Elizabeth Hospital in Singapore since 2016. After election campaign, he had planned to undergo medical check-up there, stated the DMK whip in the Assembly.

items.title: Tiruchi residents among those killed in Vietnam boat accident
items.summary: N. Balaji, A.M. Sheikh Abdullah, and S. Alagurajan, were residents of Tiruchi who were killed in the Vietnam boat accident

items.title: Poor monsoon and no Cauvery water: distress in Tamil Nadu’s delta region
items.summary: All is not well in the famed ‘Granary of South India’. Between a poor southwest  monsoon and lack of water in the Cauvery, crops and farmers are left high and dry. The kuruvai cultivation area in Thanjavur alone has dipped to around 89,000 acres this season from around 1,94,000 acres in the previous cultivation year.  This report describes the situation on the ground

items.title: Murray & Co and its Sunday auction: the famous ‘hammer’ of Madras nears the century mark
items.summary: Since February 10, 1927, the hammer at the auction house Murray & Co has not had a break except for two seasons: for a few weeks during a relocation to a new address and then during the pandemic. The operational space might have changed a few times, but its place in people’s mind space has not

items.title: VCK turns its focus to Tamil nationalism post-election
items.summary: VCK founder and Chidambaram MP Thol. Thirumavalavan has urged the party to gear up for a ‘Tamil nationalist’ public rally on August 17 in Ulundurpet near Villupuram

items.title: 99 years old,  Gem & Co and Kalathi Shop in Chennai show an enterprise need not have muscle to survive time
items.summary: As these retail outlets march towards the centenary mark, an attempt to decipher that which makes them click: Gem & Co at NSC Bose Road in George Town has persisted with its belief that fountain pens, even artisanal ones, have a place in this digital age. And Kalathi Shop, operating from a hole in the wall at North Mada Street in Mylapore, is essentially a petty shop that has people beating a path to its door for its rose milk

items.title: Chennai’s Velachery residents seek equitable water supply, replacement of ageing pipelines
items.summary: A resident says many tail-end areas in Velachery suffer erratic water supply or inadequate supply because of old network

items.title: Chennai Corporation plans holistic infra upgrade for three localities
items.summary: The civic body will improve 80 km of roads across Perambur-Vyasarpadi, Ambattur and Guindy Industrial Estates at around ₹1.18 crore; this includes transit integration and junction upgrades

items.title: Tamil Nadu government seeks assistance from Union government for MRTS revamp in Chennai
items.summary: ₹6,600-crore project is aimed at transforming  MRTS to Chennai Metro standards by refurbishing stations and upgrading trains; T.N. urges Union govt. to consider plan as ‘Central sector’ project

items.title: NEP 2020 is first serious attempt to decolonise education system, says T.N. Governor
items.summary: Pointing out that the purpose of an education is to build character the Governor said the purpose of the NEP 2020 is to create a good person

items.summary: The project is envisioned as one of India’s largest export-oriented footwear facility, catering to leading global brands such as Adidas for both domestic and world markets

items.summary: Vice-President of India C.P. Radhakrishnan praises her songs for transcending linguistic and regional boundaries, uniting millions through the universal language of music

items.title: Oncology Kitchen at ICH takes a worry off the plate for parents of children receiving  cancer treatment
items.summary: Oncology Kitchen at the Institute of Child Health and Hospital for Children in Egmore meets the huge nutritional needs of children undergoing cancer treatment. And the fact that the bill of fare comes free is a huge relief to their parents

Lives Among The Palms: Students from the palmyrah belt turn the lens on life, landscape and labour
items.summary: An exhibition curated by photographer Palani Kumar at the Lalit Kala Akademi, Lives Among The Palms features over 300 photographs captured by seven students from Ramanathapuram, as they document the landscapes, labour, and challenges around them

items.title: Tamil Nadu’s Total Fertility Rate stands at 1.3, need to study situation, says Health Minister
items.summary: K.G. Arunraj says the TFR is lower than the replacement level of 2.1, which is required to maintain population stability; situation can change if factors behind the decline are addressed, he adds

items.title: History-sheeter from Andhra Pradesh arrested for matrimonial fraud
items.summary: The accused Chella Narayanan has multiple criminal cases pending against him in Andhra Pradesh, including cases involving murder, theft, cheating, and offences under the POCSO Act

items.title: DakshinaChitra’s LangFest 2026 makes space for the pluralities of voices in history
items.summary: Through its panel discussions and artistic performances lined up for the two-day event, the fest aims to combat the idea of a single historical truth by acknowledging the multiplicities existing in history

items.title: Judge a purchase by its cover: how three sustainable outlets in Chennai make sure packaging does not leave carbon footprints
items.summary: Restore at Kottivakkam on East Coast Road offers a 2 per cent discount on purchases if the customer brings their own bag; Kazhani Native Farms in Kottivakkam has placed a cardboard box at the entrance inviting customers to drop off extra shopping bags for others to reuse; and Tula India in Adyar encourages customers to bring their own bags, and those who forget are handed handmade newspaper bags

items.title: DVAC arrests seven govt. staff in one week; seizes over ₹22 lakh in surprise checks

items.title: Ban on registration of 3,085 acres of ‘temple land’ in Karur district lifted
items.summary: In the first phase, 471 survey numbers, which were mistakenly banned from deed registration and were not part of any court cases, were reviewed and released from deed registration ban.

items.summary: On an average, about 470 tonnes of waste is generated daily in Tiruchi city and about 70% of the garbage is segregated at source, according to officials
